pattern: Every output slot is a fixed-length running sum over a circular window that either looks ahead or looks behind, so building one prefix-sum array over the code doubled onto itself turns every window sum into a single subtraction.
hints: For each position, the answer only depends on summing a fixed number of neighboring positions -- ahead of it if the shift is positive, behind it if negative, and it's simply zero if the shift is zero.
Because the array wraps around, it helps to imagine the code written out twice back to back; then every circular window becomes an ordinary contiguous slice of that doubled sequence.

Build a running prefix sum over the doubled sequence once, then each answer is just the difference of two prefix values picked out by the window's start and end -- no need to re-add the same numbers for every position.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, zero shift yields all zeros
single-element code, only valid shift is zero
largest positive shift for a two-element code
largest negative shift for a two-element code
shift equal to n - 1, wraps around every other element
shift equal to -(n - 1), wraps the other direction
all elements identical, isolates whether the count of terms is right rather than their values

stress.description: random code arrays of varying length with random valid shifts, both directions
